title: cla
summary: Clear the contents of the current axes without clearing the whole figure.

gpu_support.notes: `cla` clears plotting state only. It does not execute numeric GPU kernels.

description: `cla` clears the current axes only. This is useful when a figure contains multiple subplots and you want to clear one axes without disturbing the rest of the figure.
behaviors: The current subplot selection determines which axes are cleared.
Other axes in the same figure are left untouched.
`cla` resets the line-style cycle for the cleared axes so subsequent plots start from the default style sequence again.

examples.output: % only the second subplot is cleared

examples.description: Clear the current axes and plot again
examples.output: % the second plot starts on an empty axes

faqs.question: How is `cla` different from `clf`?
faqs.answer: `cla` clears only the current axes, while `clf` clears the entire current figure.

faqs.question: Does `cla` affect other subplots?
faqs.answer: No. It clears only the active axes.

faqs.question: Does `cla` run on the GPU?
faqs.answer: No. It only mutates host-side plotting state.

gpu_residency: `cla` only mutates plotting state and does not affect array residency.
gpu_behavior: `cla` performs no provider dispatch; it clears the active axes from the plotting registry and renderer state.
